questions.question: A right triangle has legs of length 5 and 12. Find the sine of the angle opposite the side of length 5.
questions.explanation: The hypotenuse is √(5² + 12²) = √169 = 13. Sine equals opposite/hypotenuse = 5/13.

questions.question: If cos(θ) = 3/5 and θ is in the first quadrant, what is sin(θ)?
questions.explanation: Use the Pythagorean identity sin²(θ) + cos²(θ) = 1. Then sin²(θ) = 1 − 9/25 = 16/25, so sin(θ) = 4/5 (positive in Q1).

questions.question: A ladder leans against a wall, making a 65° angle with the ground. If the ladder is 10 feet long, how far up the wall does it reach? Round to the nearest tenth.
questions.explanation: The height is the opposite side to the 65° angle. Use sin(65°) = height/10, so height = 10 × sin(65°) ≈ 10 × 0.906 ≈ 9.1.

questions.question: On the unit circle, what is the value of tan(240°)?
questions.explanation: 240° is in Q3 where both sin and cos are negative. sin(240°) = −√3/2 and cos(240°) = −1/2, so tan(240°) = (−√3/2)/(−1/2) = √3.

questions.question: Simplify: sin²(x) + sin²(x)tan²(x)
questions.explanation: Factor: sin²(x)[1 + tan²(x)]. Use the identity 1 + tan²(x) = sec²(x), so sin²(x) · sec²(x) = sin²(x)/(cos²(x)) = tan²(x).

questions.question: A surveyor measures the angle of elevation to the top of a building as 35° from a point 60 meters away. What is the height of the building? Round to the nearest metre.
questions.explanation: Use tan(35°) = height/60. Height = 60 × tan(35°) ≈ 60 × 0.700 ≈ 42 metres.
